behavior: Walks deliberately along wet shorelines and in shallow water, probing mud or sand with its bill or picking aquatic invertebrates from the surface; often forages alone and gives loud, ringing calls when disturbed or in flight.
field_marks: Large, robust shorebird with a bulky body
Long, straight, heavy dark bill, distinctly longer than the head
Gray-brown upperparts with paler underparts; breeding plumage is more mottled and barred
Bold black-and-white wing stripe exposed in flight
Short, squared tail and broad, rounded wings
habitat: Open beaches, mudflats, marshes, tidal estuaries, bayshores, and rocky coasts; breeding habitat also includes freshwater marshes, wet meadows, and grasslands near shallow wetlands.

sources.title: Willet Life History — All About Birds
